description: Derive each relational document-reference group's requiredness from jsonSchemaForInsert in the owning materialized scope and populate DocumentReferenceMapping.IsRequired with that derived value.
acceptance-criteria: The relational extraction path no longer uses raw documentPathsMapping.isRequired as the source of truth for DocumentReferenceMapping.IsRequired.
Core.ApiSchema DocumentPath.IsRequired and raw documentPathsMapping.isRequired remain unchanged for non-relational consumers such as resource load-order calculation.

The requiredness resolver walks from the owning materialized table scope to the reference object path using jsonSchemaForInsert required arrays and treats a reference group as required only when every object segment requires the next property and no traversed schema segment is x-nullable.
The resolver follows the same requiredness and x-nullable semantics used by scalar and descriptor column derivation within a materialized row.

For root, child collection, nested collection, root extension, collection-aligned extension, and extension child collection scopes, row-existence ancestors that only decide whether a row is materialized do not make columns nullable inside the materialized row.
The derived group requiredness is applied uniformly by reference binding to the reference group's DocumentId column and every propagated scalar or descriptor identity binding column.
Missing, non-object, or otherwise mismatched jsonSchemaForInsert path traversal fails fast with a diagnostic naming the resource and reference path.
The implementation is schema-driven and contains no SurveyResponse-specific, choice-name-specific, or column-name-substring rule.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/Steps/ExtractInputs/ExtractInputsStep.cs, src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/Steps/ExtractInputs/DocumentReferenceMappingsExtractor.cs, src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/Steps/ExtractInputs/ApiSchemaNodeRequirements.cs, and any helper introduced for effective reference requiredness.

Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses/ReferenceObjectPathScopeResolver.cs, src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses/ReferenceBindingPass.cs, and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses/ExtensionTableDerivationPass.cs to confirm owning-scope behavior matches the derived model.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ExtractInputsStepTests|FullyQualifiedName~ReferenceBindingRelationalModelSetPassTests|FullyQualifiedName~ExtensionTableDerivationTests"

description: Update identity-reference validation so identity-component references are checked against jsonSchemaForInsert-derived requiredness and fail fast when an identity path depends on an optional reference group.
acceptance-criteria: The identity-reference guard consumes the derived DocumentReferenceMapping.IsRequired value instead of raw documentPathsMapping.isRequired.
Compilation fails when any identityJsonPaths entry is sourced from a reference object that is not effectively required by jsonSchemaForInsert.
The failure diagnostic names the owning resource, reference object path, and identity path that made the optional identity reference invalid.
Required identity-component references that are required by jsonSchemaForInsert continue to compile and produce NOT NULL reference group columns.
The implementation does not force optional identity-reference groups to NOT NULL as a fallback.
The guard applies uniformly to references in root, collection, nested, extension, and extension child collection scopes.
steps-to-verify: Review identity-reference validation in src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/Steps/ExtractInputs/ExtractInputsStep.cs, src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/Steps/ExtractInputs/DocumentReferenceMappingsExtractor.cs, and any helper introduced for effective reference requiredness.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ExtractInputsStepTests|FullyQualifiedName~ReferenceBindingRelationalModelSetPassTests"

description: Add focused relational model unit coverage for jsonSchemaForInsert-derived reference requiredness in root, child collection, nested collection, required, optional, and x-nullable cases.
acceptance-criteria: Unit tests cover an optional choice-like reference where documentPathsMapping.isRequired is true but jsonSchemaForInsert does not require the reference object.
Unit tests assert the reference group's DocumentId and propagated identity columns are nullable when the derived reference group is optional.
Unit tests assert required references remain non-nullable when jsonSchemaForInsert requires the reference object.
Unit tests assert x-nullable on a traversed reference object path makes the reference group optional even when documentPathsMapping.isRequired is true.
Unit tests cover child collection and nested collection scopes where row-existence ancestors are ignored inside the materialized row.
Unit tests cover the optional identity-reference failure path and assert the diagnostic includes the owning resource, reference path, and identity path.
Unit tests or focused assertions confirm raw ApiSchema documentPathsMapping.isRequired values are not rewritten while relational DocumentReferenceMapping.IsRequired receives the derived value.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ExtractInputsStepTests|FullyQualifiedName~ReferenceBindingRelationalModelSetPassTests"
Run dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit

description: Add focused relational model unit coverage for jsonSchemaForInsert-derived reference requiredness inside extension scopes and for preserved reference constraints on optional reference groups.
acceptance-criteria: Unit tests cover root extension, collection-aligned extension, and extension child collection scopes where optional _ext row-presence ancestors are ignored inside the materialized extension row.
Unit tests assert extension-scope reference groups are nullable or non-nullable according to owning-scope jsonSchemaForInsert requiredness, not raw documentPathsMapping.isRequired.
Constraint or binding tests confirm optional reference groups still receive their per-reference all-or-none CHECK constraints.
Constraint or binding tests confirm optional reference groups still receive their composite FKs.
Tests confirm no choice-group at-most-one or exactly-one CHECK constraint is introduced as part of nullable reference handling.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ExtensionTableDerivationTests|FullyQualifiedName~ConstraintDerivationRelationalModelSetPassTests|FullyQualifiedName~ReferenceBindingRelationalModelSetPassTests"
Run dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit

description: Add a checked-in authoritative DS52+TPDM generated fixture and register it with the DDL golden test suite.
acceptance-criteria: A checked-in authoritative DS52+TPDM fixture directory exists under src/dms/backend/Fixtures/authoritative using a clear name such as ds-5.2-tpdm.
The fixture.json loads the existing DS 5.2 core authoritative ApiSchema plus a real TPDM ApiSchema input, declares both pgsql and mssql dialects, and emits a DDL manifest.

The TPDM ApiSchema input is checked in under the fixture inputs directory or referenced through the existing repo: fixture input path convention; fixture execution does not depend on bin output or a hand-written SurveyResponse-only schema shortcut.
The existing ds-5.2 core-only and sample-extension fixtures are not treated as equivalent unless TPDM is actually part of their fixture.json.

Inspection of the fixture's SurveyResponse resource schema verifies the responder-choice document reference mappings reproduce the DMS-1206 mismatch: raw documentPathsMapping.isRequired is true while jsonSchemaForInsert does not require any responder choice branch.

src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/AuthoritativeDdlGoldenTests.cs, or an equivalent authoritative golden test registration, includes a named DS52+TPDM fixture so both PostgreSQL and SQL Server outputs are generated and compared.
steps-to-verify: Inspect src/dms/backend/Fixtures/authoritative/ds-5.2-tpdm/fixture.json, or the selected DS52+TPDM fixture directory, and its inputs to confirm the fixture is real, checked in, includes TPDM, and declares pgsql and mssql.
Inspect the SurveyResponse resourceSchema in the DS52+TPDM fixture input to confirm the DMS-1206 requiredness mismatch is present.
Inspect src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/AuthoritativeDdlGoldenTests.cs for a named DS52+TPDM authoritative test fixture.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "Category=Authoritative"

description: Update the DS52+TPDM authoritative relational model and DDL goldens so SurveyResponse responder-choice reference groups are nullable in both PostgreSQL and SQL Server outputs.
acceptance-criteria: The DS52+TPDM fixture's expected relational-model.pgsql.manifest.json marks the SurveyResponderChoiceContact_*, SurveyResponderChoiceStaff_*, and SurveyResponderChoiceStudent_* reference group columns nullable on edfi.SurveyResponse.
The DS52+TPDM fixture's expected relational-model.mssql.manifest.json marks the same SurveyResponse reference group columns nullable.
The DS52+TPDM fixture's expected pgsql.sql emits those SurveyResponse columns without NOT NULL.
The DS52+TPDM fixture's expected mssql.sql emits those SurveyResponse columns without NOT NULL.
The existing per-reference all-or-none CHECK constraints remain present for the contact, staff, and student responder-choice reference groups.
The existing composite FKs remain present for the contact, staff, and student responder-choice reference groups.
No at-most-one or exactly-one choice CHECK constraint is added for SurveyResponse responder choices.
Related effective-schema and DDL manifests are updated when the generated fixture hash, statement counts, or inventories change.
steps-to-verify: Run UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "Category=Authoritative"
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "Category=Authoritative"

Inspect the DS52+TPDM expected relational-model.pgsql.manifest.json, relational-model.mssql.manifest.json, pgsql.sql, and mssql.sql SurveyResponse sections for nullable choice-reference columns, unchanged all-or-none CHECKs, unchanged composite FKs, and absence of choice-group CHECKs.

description: Wire the DS52+TPDM authoritative fixture into the API-level integration test harness.
acceptance-criteria: The API-level integration harness exposes a FixtureKey such as AuthoritativeDs52Tpdm that resolves to the DS52+TPDM fixture directory.
FixtureContext grants CRUD access to SurveyResponse and the prerequisite resources used by the SurveyResponse scenarios through the same resource enumeration path used by existing fixtures.
The PostgreSQL and SQL Server baseline caches can provision the DS52+TPDM fixture from the same materialized fixture context used by the in-process DMS host.
The integration test README fixture map documents the DS52+TPDM fixture key, source repo path, and intended use for SurveyResponse/TPDM production-shape regressions.
No existing integration fixture key is repointed from DS52 core-only or sample-extension schema to DS52+TPDM.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Fixtures/FixtureContext.cs, src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Fixtures/FixtureRepositoryPaths.cs, and src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/README.md for the new fixture key mapping.

Review src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Fixtures/FixtureContextLoader.cs, src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Postgresql/PostgresqlBaselineCache.cs, and src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Mssql/MssqlBaselineCache.cs to confirm the DS52+TPDM key uses the same materialized fixture and generated-DDL baseline paths as existing fixtures.

description: Build the shared DS52+TPDM SurveyResponse API-level integration scenario, including prerequisite setup and payload builders for responder-choice variants.
acceptance-criteria: A shared SurveyResponse scenario prepares the minimum referenced Survey, Contact, Staff, Student, descriptor, calendar/session, and education organization prerequisites needed for reference and descriptor validity.

The scenario exposes cases or payload builders for SurveyResponse with only the contact responder choice branch, only the staff responder choice branch, only the student responder choice branch, and no responder choice branch.
Prerequisite setup follows existing API-level integration-test harness patterns and does not bypass the SurveyResponse API path under test.
The scenario uses unique identity values per case and does not depend on test order.
The scenario provides a dialect-neutral assertion hook, where practical, for verifying absent responder-choice branch columns persist as NULL.
steps-to-verify: Review the new SurveyResponse scenario under src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Scenarios against existing shared scenario patterns.
Run dotnet build src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj

description: Add PostgreSQL and SQL Server API-level relational integration wrappers that run the DS52+TPDM SurveyResponse responder-choice scenario.
acceptance-criteria: The PostgreSQL wrapper binds the shared SurveyResponse scenario to FixtureKey.AuthoritativeDs52Tpdm or the selected DS52+TPDM fixture key.
The SQL Server wrapper binds the same shared scenario to the DS52+TPDM fixture key and skips only when ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin is not configured.
The contact-only, staff-only, student-only, and no-responder-choice SurveyResponse POST cases each receive a successful create response.
The tests assert the API path does not return the unrecognized relational write failure.
Where practical, the wrappers assert absent responder-choice branch columns persist as NULL on the generated relational table.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "Category=PostgresqlIntegration&FullyQualifiedName~SurveyResponse"

When SQL Server is configured, run dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "Category=MssqlIntegration&FullyQualifiedName~SurveyResponse"

Review src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Tests/Postgresql and src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Tests/Mssql to confirm both wrappers use the DS52+TPDM fixture key.

description: Run cross-dialect generated DDL smoke and compatibility coverage for the DS52+TPDM fixture after the nullable reference model and goldens are updated.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL generated DDL smoke or compatibility tests apply the DS52+TPDM authoritative DDL successfully.
SQL Server generated DDL smoke or compatibility tests apply the DS52+TPDM authoritative DDL successfully when ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin points at a running SQL Server.
The DS52+TPDM generated DDL records dms.EffectiveSchema correctly and passes the same compatibility-gate expectations used by existing authoritative fixtures.
No DDL smoke failure is introduced by nullable SurveyResponse choice-reference columns, all-or-none CHECKs, or composite FKs.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Authoritative"
If dedicated DS52+TPDM smoke or compatibility classes are added, also run the same PostgreSQL project with --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Tpdm".
Verify SQL Server is listening and ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin is set, or start a temporary SQL Server container as described in AGENTS.md.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Authoritative"
If dedicated DS52+TPDM smoke or compatibility classes are added, also run the same SQL Server project with --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Tpdm".

description: Medium: Remove unrelated authorization demo and DMS-1207 design artifacts from the DMS-1206 branch.
acceptance-criteria: The DMS-1206 branch diff no longer includes authorization-relational-demo.http.
The DMS-1206 branch diff no longer includes DMS-1207 authorization epic and JIRA-index documentation changes unless they are intentionally moved to a separate DMS-1207 branch or PR.
No checked-in DMS-1206 artifact contains local absolute paths, fixed demo credentials, or database passwords unrelated to the SurveyResponse nullability fix.
The remaining branch diff is limited to DMS-1206 implementation, tests, fixtures, and development tracking artifacts.
steps-to-verify: Run git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD and confirm authorization-relational-demo.http, reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/14-authorization/21-token-info-relational-auth-schema.md, reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/14-authorization/EPIC.md, and reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/JIRA-INDEX.md are absent from the DMS-1206 diff or have been moved to a separate DMS-1207 change.

Run git diff origin/main...HEAD -- authorization-relational-demo.http reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/14-authorization reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/JIRA-INDEX.md and confirm the DMS-1206 branch has no unrelated demo credential, database password, local-path, or DMS-1207 documentation artifact.
Review git diff origin/main...HEAD to confirm remaining changes map to DMS-1206 SurveyResponse relational reference nullability coverage.

description: Low: Consolidate jsonSchemaForInsert requiredness parsing used by scalar, extension, and document-reference nullability derivation.
acceptance-criteria: A shared helper owns JSON Schema required-array parsing and x-nullable boolean parsing for relational nullability decisions.

EffectiveReferenceRequirednessResolver, DeriveColumnsAndBindDescriptorEdgesStep, and ExtensionTableDerivationPass call the shared helper instead of maintaining separate copies of the same parsing logic.
The helper preserves existing path-specific diagnostics for malformed required arrays, malformed required entries, and malformed x-nullable values.
Existing reference requiredness behavior remains unchanged for root, collection, nested collection, root extension, collection-aligned extension, and extension child collection cases.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/Steps/ExtractInputs/EffectiveReferenceRequirednessResolver.cs, src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/Steps/DeriveColumnsAndBindDescriptorEdgesStep.cs, and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses/ExtensionTableDerivationPass.cs to confirm required/x-nullable parsing is shared.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~CoreReferenceRequiredness|FullyQualifiedName~ExtensionReferenceRequiredness|FullyQualifiedName~ReferenceBindingRelationalModelSetPassTests"
Run dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit
